question: Which of the following main industry/industries was/were developed in the second industrial revolution that took place after about 1850 ?

en.content: Which of the following main industry/industries was/were developed in the second industrial revolution that took place after about 1850 ?
en.explanation:
The hallmark industries of the Second Industrial Revolution (post-1850) were the chemical and electrical sectors.

Coal & iron, cotton spinning & weaving, and steam engines all trace back to the First Industrial Revolution (late 18th–early 19th century).

The rise of large-scale chemical plants (dyes, fertilizers, explosives) and electrical engineering (telegraphy, electric lighting, motors) defines the period after 1850.

Answer: Option 3 — Chemical and electrical.

hi.content: दूसरी औद्योगिक क्रांति में, जो लगभग 1850 के बाद हुई थी, निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा/कौन-से समूह तकनीक/उद्योगों का विकास हुआ था?

options.content: कपास की कताई और बुनाई (Cotton spinning and weaving)

hi.explanation:
द्वितीय औद्योगिक क्रांति (1850 के बाद) की मुख्य विशेषता रासायनिक तथा विद्युत क्षेत्र थे।

कोयला एवं लोहा, कपास कताई एवं बुनाई, तथा भाप इंजन का विकास पहली औद्योगिक क्रांति (अठारहवीं शताब्दी के अंतिम चरण से उन्नीसवीं शताब्दी के प्रारम्भिक वर्षों) से संबंधित है।

बड़े पैमाने पर रासायनिक संयंत्रों (रंग, उर्वरक, विस्फोटक) एवं विद्युत अभियांत्रिकी (टेलीग्राफी, विद्युत प्रकाश, मोटर) का उन्नयन 1850 के बाद के काल की विशेषता है।

उत्तर: विकल्प 3 — रासायनिक और विद्युत।

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: History • General Studies
en.excerpt: Which of the following main industry/industries was/were developed in the second industrial revolution that took place after about 1850 ?

question: Which among the following materials was used in making the Harappan seals ?

en.content: Which among the following materials was used in making the Harappan seals ?
en.explanation:
The Harappan seals were almost universally carved from steatite. This soft stone could be easily inscribed with animal motifs and script, then fired to harden into durable seals.

Answer: Option 4 – Steatite

hi.content: हड़प्पा मुहरों (Seals) के निर्माण में, निम्नलिखित में से किस सामग्री का उपयोग किया गया था?
hi.explanation:
हड़प्पा की मुद्राएँ प्रायः स्टीटाइट पत्थर से बनाई जाती थीं। यह एक मुलायम पत्थर है, जिस पर पशु आकृतियाँ और लेख अंकित करना सरल था, और फिर इसे पकाने पर यह कठोर तथा टिकाऊ मुद्राओं में परिवर्तित हो जाता था।

उत्तर: विकल्प 4 – स्टीटाइट

question: Which one among the following mahajanapadas in ancient India was an oligarchy ?

en.content: Which one among the following mahajanapadas in ancient India was an oligarchy ?
en.explanation:
The only oligarchic (gana-sangha) mahājanapada among the four was Vajji.

Vajji (also called the Vajjika League) was governed by a council of clan-elders—an aristocratic oligarchy.

Kosala, Gandhara and Magadha were all monarchies ruled by kings.

hi.content: प्राचीन भारत में, निम्नलिखित में से किस महाजनपद में समूह-शासन (Oligarchy) था?
hi.explanation:
चार महाजनपदों में केवल वज्जि (गणसंग) एक कुलीनतंत्र (oligarchic) महाजनपद था।

वज्जि (जिसे वज्जिक संघ भी कहा जाता है) का शासन कुल-प्रमुखों की परिषद् द्वारा किया जाता था—यह एक कुलीन कुलतंत्र था।

कोशल, गांधार और मगध सभी राजतंत्र थे, जिनका शासन राजाओं द्वारा किया जाता था।

solution:
The only oligarchic (gana-sangha) mahājanapada among the four was Vajji.

question: Which one of the following sculptures found at Sanchi Stupa is NOT directly inspired by Buddhist ideas ?

en.content: Which one of the following sculptures found at Sanchi Stupa is NOT directly inspired by Buddhist ideas ?
en.explanation:
The sculpture not drawn from Buddhist iconography is the Shalbhanjika (Option 2).

Empty seat → aniconic symbol of the Buddha

Tree → represents the Bodhi tree under which the Buddha attained enlightenment

Wheel → the Dharma‐chakra, symbolizing the Buddha’s teaching

Shalbhanjika → a Yakshi or “tree maiden” figure from pre‐Buddhist fertility cults, not a Buddhist idea

hi.content: साँची स्तूप में पाई गई निम्नलिखित मूर्तिकला (Sculptures) में से कौन-सी, प्रस्तर-शिल्प विचारों से प्रेरित नहीं है?
hi.explanation:
बौद्ध प्रतिमाशास्त्र से नहीं ली गई मूर्ति शालभंजिका (विकल्प 2) है।

खाली आसन → बुद्ध का अनाकार प्रतीक (एनाइकोनिक प्रतीक)

वृक्ष → बोधिवृक्ष का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है, जिसके नीचे बुद्ध को ज्ञान प्राप्त हुआ था

चक्र → धर्मचक्र, बुद्ध के उपदेश का प्रतीक

शालभंजिका → एक यक्षी या "वृक्ष-नारी" आकृति, जो पूर्व-बौद्ध प्रजनन (उर्वरता) संस्कारों से संबंधित है, कोई बौद्ध अवधारणा नहीं है

question: In British India, which one among the following Acts permitted detention without trial for up to two years ?

en.content: In British India, which one among the following Acts permitted detention without trial for up to two years ?
en.explanation:
The Act in question is the Rowlatt Act of 1919. Here’s a quick breakdown:

• Name: Anarchical and Revolutionary Crimes Act, 1919 (commonly “Rowlatt Act”)

• Key provision: allowed the government to detain suspects without trial for up to two years

• Other powers it granted:

– House-to-house searches without warrant

– Press censorship

• Impact: widespread anger that led to protests (notably the Jallianwala Bagh massacre)

None of the other listed Acts (Regulating Act 1773, Pitt’s India Act 1784, Government of India Act 1935) included a two-year detention-without-trial clause.

hi.content: ब्रिटिश भारत में, निम्नलिखित में से किस अधिनियम के अंतर्गत विचारण के बिना दो वर्ष की अवधि तक निरुद्ध रखना अनुमत था?
hi.explanation:
यहाँ उल्लिखित अधिनियम 1919 का रॉलेट अधिनियम (Rowlatt Act) है। यहाँ इसका संक्षिप्त विवरण प्रस्तुत है:

• नाम: अराजक और क्रांतिकारी अपराध अधिनियम, 1919 (सामान्यतः 'रॉलेट अधिनियम')

• मुख्य प्रावधान: सरकार को संदेहास्पद व्यक्तियों को बिना मुकदमे के दो वर्ष तक नजरबंद रखने की अनुमति देता था।

• अन्य अधिकार जो प्रदान किए गए:

– घर-घर तलाशी बिना वारंट के

– प्रेस पर सेंसरशिप

• प्रभाव: व्यापक असंतोष, जिस कारण विरोध प्रदर्शन हुए (विशेषतः जलियाँवाला बाग हत्याकांड)

अन्य उल्लिखित अधिनियमों (रेग्युलेटिंग एक्ट 1773, पिट्स इंडिया एक्ट 1784, भारत सरकार अधिनियम 1935) में बिना मुकदमे के दो वर्ष तक नजरबंदी का प्रावधान नहीं था।

options: The target was to get one-sixth of cultivable land in India as donation
It was approved through a Central Government Act
It was ensured that the donated land was free from all litigation
The first donation of land was in Bihar
question: Which one of the following statements about the Bhoodan Movement is correct ?

en.content: Which one of the following statements about the Bhoodan Movement is correct ?

options.content: The target was to get one-sixth of cultivable land in India as donation
It was approved through a Central Government Act
It was ensured that the donated land was free from all litigation
The first donation of land was in Bihar

en.explanation:
Option 1 is correct.

Vinoba Bhave’s Bhoodan Movement (from 1951 on) set itself the goal of securing voluntary gifts equal to one-sixth of India’s cultivable land for redistribution to landless peasants.

hi.content: भूदान आंदोलन के बारे में निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही है?

options.content: भारत में कृषि योग्य भूमि का छठवां भाग भूमिहीनों के बीच प्राप्त करने का लक्ष्य था
इसे केंद्रीय सरकार के अधिनियम के माध्यम से अनुमोदित किया गया था
यह सुनिश्चित किया गया था कि दान दी गई भूमि सभी प्रकार के मुकदमों से मुक्त है
भूमि का पहला दान बिहार में हुआ था

hi.explanation:
विकल्प 1 सही है।

विनोबा भावे का भूदान आंदोलन (1951 से प्रारंभ) का उद्देश्य भूमिहीन कृषकों को पुनः वितरण के लिए भारत की cultivable भूमि के छठवें हिस्से के बराबर स्वैच्छिक दान प्राप्त करना था।

question:
Which of the following statements about Srimanta Shankardeva is/are correct ?
1.In the late fifteenth century Shankardeva emerged as one of the leading proponents of Vaishnavism in Assam
2.He was the founder of Gaudiya Vaishnavism
3.His teachings are known as Bhagavati Dharma
4.He encouraged the establishment of satra and naam ghar
Select the answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Which of the following statements about Srimanta Shankardeva is/are correct ?
1.In the late fifteenth century Shankardeva emerged as one of the leading proponents of Vaishnavism in Assam
2.He was the founder of Gaudiya Vaishnavism
3.His teachings are known as Bhagavati Dharma
4.He encouraged the establishment of satra and naam ghar
Select the answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
The only correct statements are 1, 3 and 4.

So the answer is Option 3.

Briefly:

• Shankardev began propagating neo-Vaishnavism in Assam by the late 15th century.

• He did ​not​ found Gaudiya Vaishnavism (that was Caitanya Mahaprabhu’s).

• His own teachings are known as “Bhagavata” (or Ekasarana) Dharma.

• He set up both satras (monastic centres) and naam ghars (prayer-houses).

hi.content:
श्रीमंत शंकरदेव के संबंध में, निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा/कौन-से कथन सही है/हैं?
1.पंद्रहवीं शताब्दी के उत्तरार्द्ध में शंकरदेव का आविर्भाव असम में वैष्णववाद के अग्रणी प्रवर्तक (Proponent) के रूप में हुआ
2.वे गौड़ीय वैष्णववाद के संस्थापक थे
3.उनकी शिक्षाएं भगवती धर्म के रूप में प्रचलित हैं
4.उन्होंने नाम और नाम घर स्थापित करने को प्रोत्साहित किया
नीचे दिए गए कूट का प्रयोग कर उत्तर चुनिए:
hi.explanation:
केवल कथन 1, 3 और 4 सही हैं।

इसलिए उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

संक्षेप में:

• शंकरदेव ने पंद्रहवीं शताब्दी के उत्तरार्ध में असम में नव-वैष्णववाद का प्रचार करना प्रारंभ किया।

• उन्होंने गौड़ीय वैष्णववाद की स्थापना नहीं की (गौड़ीय वैष्णववाद की स्थापना चैतन्य महाप्रभु ने की थी)।

• उनकी शिक्षाएँ 'भागवत' (या एकशरण) धर्म के रूप में जानी जाती हैं।

• उन्होंने सत्‍त्र (मठ केंद्र) और नामघर (प्रार्थना गृह) दोनों की स्थापना की।

उत्तर: विकल्प 2 (मसुलीपटम)

en.content:
Chronologically arrange the following political events related to Indian National Movement beginning from the earliest

Lucknow Pact – 1916

question: Which among the following Acts provided for the establishment of a Supreme Court of Justice at Calcutta for Europeans, their employees and the citizens of India ?

en.content: Which among the following Acts provided for the establishment of a Supreme Court of Justice at Calcutta for Europeans, their employees and the citizens of India ?
en.explanation:
The correct choice is Option 1: the Regulating Act of 1773.

Key point:

• Section 36 of the Regulating Act, passed in 1773, set up the Supreme Court of Judicature at Fort William (Calcutta) in 1774 to serve Europeans, Company servants and “Indian subjects” living in Bengal.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से किस अधिनियम में, यूरोप के लोगों, उनके कर्मचारियों और भारत के नागरिकों के लिए कलकत्ता में न्याय के उपयुक्त न्यायालय स्थापित किए जाने का उपबंध किया गया?
hi.explanation:
सही विकल्प है विकल्प 1: विनियामक अधिनियम, 1773।

question: Who, among the following, has introduced a resolution in 1882 which is also called the Magna Carta of Local Self-Government in India ?

en.content: Who, among the following, has introduced a resolution in 1882 which is also called the Magna Carta of Local Self-Government in India ?
en.explanation:
The 1882 resolution known as the “Magna Carta of Local Self-Government in India” was introduced by Lord Ripon.

Key points:

It formally encouraged the creation of elected local bodies (municipalities and district boards).

It granted them greater autonomy over local affairs (e.g. public health, sanitation, roads).

It marked a major step toward involving Indians in local administration.

Correct answer: Lord Ripon (Option 3).

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से किसने 1882 में एक संहिता प्रस्तुत किया था, जिसे भारत में स्थानीय स्व-शासन का मील का पत्थर कहा जाता है?
hi.explanation:
सन् 1882 का वह प्रस्ताव जिसे "मग्ना कार्टा ऑफ़ लोकल सेल्फ-गवर्नमेंट इन इंडिया" कहा जाता है, लॉर्ड रिपन द्वारा प्रस्तुत किया गया था।

मुख्य बिंदु:

इसने औपचारिक रूप से निर्वाचित स्थानीय निकायों (नगरपालिकाएँ और जिला बोर्ड) के गठन को प्रोत्साहित किया।

इन निकायों को स्थानीय मामलों (जैसे- सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य, सफाई, सड़कें) पर अधिक स्वायत्तता दी गई।

यह भारतीयों को स्थानीय प्रशासन में सम्मिलित करने की दिशा में एक महत्त्वपूर्ण कदम था।

सही उत्तर: लॉर्ड रिपन (विकल्प 3)।

Briefly:

• Jizya – a tax levied on non-Muslims

• Ijma – scholarly consensus in Islamic law

• Muqaddam – a deputy or representative (sometimes used in Sufi orders but not the key disciple term)

• Murid – literally “one who aspires,” i.e. a Sufi novice or disciple under a spiritual guide

question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Taxila was the capital of which one among the following Mahajanapadas of ancient India?

en.content: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Taxila was the capital of which one among the following Mahajanapadas of ancient India?
en.explanation:
Taxila was the capital of Gandhar.

Here's a breakdown of the capitals for the given Mahajanapadas:

  1. Kuru: The capital of Kuru Mahajanapada was Indraprastha.

  2. Kasi: The capital of Kasi Mahajanapada was Varanasi (also known as Kasi).

  3. Gandhar: The capital of Gandhar Mahajanapada was Taxila (also known as Takshashila). This region was strategically located on the northwestern frontier and was an important center for trade and learning.

  4. Avanti: The capital of Avanti Mahajanapada was Ujjain (or Mahishmati).

Therefore, Taxila was the capital of Gandhar.

question: Who among the following was the last Governor-General of India before the office was permanently abolished?

en.content: Who among the following was the last Governor-General of India before the office was permanently abolished?
en.explanation:
1. The office of Governor-General of India existed both under British rule and for a short period after India’s independence on 15 August 1947.

  2. After independence, the first Governor-General was Lord Mountbatten (15 August 1947 to 21 June 1948).

  3. He was succeeded by Chakravarti Rajagopalachari, who served from 21 June 1948 until 26 January 1950.

  4. On 26 January 1950, India became a republic and the office of Governor-General was abolished, being replaced by the President of India.

Answer: Chakravarti Rajagopalachari (Option 2)

question: Which one of the following statements about the Industrial Revolution is correct?

en.content: Which one of the following statements about the Industrial Revolution is correct?

options.content: James Watt's invention converted the steam engine from being a mere pump into one which would provide energy to power machines in factories
Mathew Boulton discovered the technique of refining gold of impurities

en.explanation:
The correct choice is Option 3.

James Watt’s key innovation (the separate condenser and improvements to generate rotary motion) transformed the steam engine from a simple pump into a prime mover capable of powering factory machinery.

question: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->The first cotton mill in British India which began production in 1856 was set up by

en.content: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->The first cotton mill in British India which began production in 1856 was set up by
en.explanation:
The first cotton mill in British India, which began production in 1856, was set up by C. N. Davar.

He established the "Bombay Spinning and Weaving Company" in 1854, and it commenced production two years later, marking a significant milestone in India's industrial history.

en.content: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Inspired by the Dandi March by Mahatma Gandhi, in Tamilnadu, C Rajagopalachari led a salt march from

options.content: Trichinopoly to Vedaranniyam on Tanjore coast

en.explanation:
Inspired by Mahatma Gandhi's Dandi March, C. Rajagopalachari led a similar salt march in Tamil Nadu. The correct route for his march was:

Option 3: Trichinopoly to Vedaranniyam on Tanjore coast

This historic march is often referred to as the Vedaranyam Salt Satyagraha. It began from Trichinopoly (modern-day Tiruchirappalli) on April 13, 1930, and concluded at Vedaranyam, a coastal town in the Tanjore (now Thanjavur) district, where participants symbolically broke the salt laws by making salt from seawater.

question: Domingo Paes describes a king in these words: 'Of medium height, and of fair complexion and good figure, rather fat than thin; he has on his face signs of smallpox'. Identify the king from the options given below.

en.content: Domingo Paes describes a king in these words: 'Of medium height, and of fair complexion and good figure, rather fat than thin; he has on his face signs of smallpox'. Identify the king from the options given below.
en.explanation: The king in question is Krishnadeva Raya (Option 1). Paes visited Vijayanagar during Krishnadeva Raya’s reign (1509–1530) and describes him exactly as “of medium height, fair complexion, good figure, rather fat than thin, with signs of smallpox on his face.”

hi.content: डोमिंगो पेस ने एक राजा का वर्णन इन शब्दों में किया है: 'मध्यम कद का, गोरे रंग का और अच्छे कद-काठी का, दुबला होने की अपेक्षा मोटा; उसके चेहरे पर चेचक के निशान हैं।' नीचे दिए गए विकल्पों में से उस राजा की पहचान करें।
hi.explanation: प्रश्न में उल्लिखित राजा कृष्णदेव राय (विकल्प 1) हैं। पेस विजयनगर साम्राज्य में कृष्णदेव राय के शासनकाल (1509–1530) के दौरान आए थे और उन्होंने उनका वर्णन ठीक इस प्रकार किया है: "मध्यम कद, गोरा रंग, अच्छा शरीर, पतला नहीं बल्कि थोड़ा मोटा, चेहरे पर चेचक के दाग।"

en.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
en.subTitle: History • General Studies

question: Which of the following is NOT a part of the five principles enshrined in the 'Panchsheel' agreement signed by India and China in 1954 ?

en.content: Which of the following is NOT a part of the five principles enshrined in the 'Panchsheel' agreement signed by India and China in 1954 ?
en.explanation:
The five principles of Panchsheel are

Mutual respect for each other’s territorial integrity and sovereignty

Mutual non-aggression

Mutual non-interference in each other’s internal affairs

Equality and mutual benefit

Peaceful coexistence

“Cultural cooperation” is not one of them. Answer: Option 4.

question:
Consider the following statements about the emergence of urban centres in India from circa 6th Century BCE :

1. All of them developed away from the capitals of mahajanapadas.

2. Major towns were located along routes of communication.

3. Many were bustling centres of commercial, cultural and political activity.

How many of the above statements is/are correct ?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about the emergence of urban centres in India from circa 6th Century BCE :

1. All of them developed away from the capitals of mahajanapadas.

2. Major towns were located along routes of communication.

3. Many were bustling centres of commercial, cultural and political activity.

How many of the above statements is/are correct ?
en.explanation:
Let's analyze each of the given statements about the emergence of urban centres in India from circa 6th Century BCE:

Statement 1: "All of them developed away from the capitals of mahajanapadas."

This statement is not entirely accurate. Some urban centres did develop in proximity to the capitals of mahajanapadas, while others emerged independently. Therefore, this statement is not correct in a blanket sense.

Statement 2: "Major towns were located along routes of communication."

This statement is correct. Many major towns and urban centres were strategically located along important trade routes, which facilitated commerce, communication, and connectivity.

Statement 3: "Many were bustling centres of commercial, cultural, and political activity."

This statement is also correct. Urban centres in ancient India were often hubs of extensive commercial, cultural, and political activities. They thrived on trade and served as focal points for cultural dissemination and political administration.

Based on the above analysis:

Statement 1 is incorrect.

Statements 2 and 3 are correct.

Therefore, the correct option is Option B: 2, as two out of the three statements are correct.

question:
Consider the following statements about ancient Indian inscriptions :

1. The earliest inscriptions are in Sanskrit.

2. Kharosthi script, used in inscriptions in the north-west, was deciphered with the help of coins of Indo-Greek kings who ruled over the area.

3. Most of the inscriptions mention grand, unique events, and routine agricultural practices do not find mention.

How many of the above statements is/are correct ?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about ancient Indian inscriptions :

1. The earliest inscriptions are in Sanskrit.

2. Kharosthi script, used in inscriptions in the north-west, was deciphered with the help of coins of Indo-Greek kings who ruled over the area.

3. Most of the inscriptions mention grand, unique events, and routine agricultural practices do not find mention.

How many of the above statements is/are correct ?
en.explanation:
Let's analyze the given statements about ancient Indian inscriptions:

1. The earliest inscriptions are in Sanskrit.

This statement is incorrect. The earliest inscriptions in India are not in Sanskrit but in Prakrit, which was the vernacular language of the people. For example, the Ashokan inscriptions, dated to the 3rd century BCE, are primarily in Prakrit.

2. Kharosthi script, used in inscriptions in the north-west, was deciphered with the help of coins of Indo-Greek kings who ruled over the area.

This statement is correct. The Kharosthi script, used in ancient inscriptions in the north-western part of India (particularly in regions like present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan), was indeed deciphered with the help of bilingual coins issued by the Indo-Greek kings. These coins had inscriptions in both Greek and Kharosthi, which facilitated the process of decipherment.

3. Most of the inscriptions mention grand, unique events, and routine agricultural practices do not find mention.

This statement is also correct. Inscriptions typically record significant events such as royal proclamations, achievements, donations, and religious matters. Routine activities like agricultural practices are rarely mentioned in these inscriptions.

Now, let's count the correct statements:

- Statement 1 is incorrect.

- Statements 2 and 3 are correct.

So, the number of correct statements is 2. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option B

2

question:
Which of the following statements are correct?

1. British 'trade surplus' with India in the nineteenth century meant that the value of British exports to India was much higher than the value of British imports from India.

2. India played a crucial role in the late-nineteenth-century world economy by helping Britain balance its deficits.

3. Britain grew opium in India and exported it to China and, therefore, for a while after the 1820s, opium became India's single largest export.

4. The nineteenth century saw export of Indian raw materials decline, and that of manufactured goods increase.

Select the answer using the code given below:

en.content:
Which of the following statements are correct?

1. British 'trade surplus' with India in the nineteenth century meant that the value of British exports to India was much higher than the value of British imports from India.

2. India played a crucial role in the late-nineteenth-century world economy by helping Britain balance its deficits.

3. Britain grew opium in India and exported it to China and, therefore, for a while after the 1820s, opium became India's single largest export.

4. The nineteenth century saw export of Indian raw materials decline, and that of manufactured goods increase.

Select the answer using the code given below:
en.explanation:
To determine which statements are correct, let's analyze each one individually:

Statement 1: "British 'trade surplus' with India in the nineteenth century meant that the value of British exports to India was much higher than the value of British imports from India."

This statement is incorrect. A trade surplus means that the value of exports exceeds the value of imports. In the case of India in the nineteenth century, Britain had a trade surplus with India because the value of British imports (i.e., raw materials like cotton, tea, and opium) from India was much higher than the value of British exports (mostly manufactured goods) to India. This trade surplus allowed Britain to finance its trade deficits with other countries.

Statement 2: "India played a crucial role in the late-nineteenth-century world economy by helping Britain balance its deficits."

This statement is correct. India indeed played a significant role in supporting Britain's balance of payments. The trade surplus with India provided a critical source of revenue for Britain, which helped it to manage its deficits with other countries.

Statement 3: "Britain grew opium in India and exported it to China and, therefore, for a while after the 1820s, opium became India's single largest export."

This statement is correct. The British colonial administration grew opium in India and exported it to China. Opium became a significant export commodity for India during the nineteenth century, especially after the 1820s, and played a key role in the economic interactions between Britain, India, and China.

Statement 4: "The nineteenth century saw export of Indian raw materials decline, and that of manufactured goods increase."

This statement is incorrect. In the nineteenth century, there was an increase in the export of Indian raw materials, such as cotton, tea, and opium, to Britain. At the same time, Indian manufactured goods faced stiff competition from British manufactured goods, leading to a decline in the export of Indian manufactured goods.

Based on this analysis, the correct statements are 2 and 3. Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: 1, 2, and 3 only

question:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:
List-I
(Unit in use in early India)		 List-II
(Content)
A. Muhurta 2. Measure of time
B. Raktika 1. Measure of weight
C. Angula 4. Measure of length
D. Pada 3. Metre of poetry

en.content:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:
List-I
(Unit in use in early India)		 List-II
(Content)
A. Muhurta 2. Measure of time
B. Raktika 1. Measure of weight
C. Angula 4. Measure of length
D. Pada 3. Metre of poetry
en.explanation:
To solve the given matching problem, we need to correctly align the units from List-I with their corresponding content descriptions from List-II. Let's analyze each unit and its logical match:

A. Muhurta: Muhurta is a traditional Hindu unit of time. Hence, it corresponds to 2. Measure of time.

B. Raktika: Raktika is an ancient Indian unit of weight, often used in weighing precious substances like gold. Thus, it should match with 1. Measure of weight.

C. Angula: Angula is an ancient unit of length in India, where it is often translated as 'finger.' Therefore, it aligns with 4. Measure of length.

D. Pada: Pada is a Sanskrit term that often refers to a specific foot or segment of a verse in poetry, thus it is associated with 3. Metre of poetry.

Based on the above analysis, the correct matching should be:

A - 2, B - 1, C - 4, D - 3

This corresponds to Option A. Here is the final matching and the correct answer:

Option A:

A B C D
2 1 4 3

question:
First coins in Indian history bearing the names and images of rulers were issued by the :

A. Mauryas

B. Pushyabhutis

C. Guptas

D. Indo-Greeks

en.content:
First coins in Indian history bearing the names and images of rulers were issued by the :

A. Mauryas

B. Pushyabhutis

C. Guptas

D. Indo-Greeks
en.explanation:
The first coins in Indian history bearing the names and images of rulers were issued by the Indo-Greeks. This issuance marked a significant development in the numismatic history of India.

The Indo-Greeks were successors of Alexander the Great's empire in the northwestern parts of the Indian subcontinent. They contributed significantly to the coinage system, introducing the practice of placing a ruler’s image and name on the coins, which was a break from the earlier punch-marked coinage system used by Indian dynasties including the Mauryas.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D :

Indo-Greeks

question:
Social theorists in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries emphasised the emergence of industrialisation, urbanisation, secularisation and bureaucratisation as hallmarks of:

A. Modernity

B. Feudalism

C. Kingship

D. Medievalism

en.content:
Social theorists in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries emphasised the emergence of industrialisation, urbanisation, secularisation and bureaucratisation as hallmarks of:

A. Modernity

B. Feudalism

C. Kingship

D. Medievalism
en.explanation:
Social theorists in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries emphasised the emergence of industrialisation, urbanisation, secularisation, and bureaucratisation as hallmarks of:

A. Modernity

B. Feudalism

C. Kingship

D. Medievalism

The correct answer is: A. Modernity

Explanation:

Modernity refers to a period marked by a shift from agrarian economies to industrial economies, leading to profound changes in the social, cultural, and economic landscape. This period includes the following key features:

  • Industrialisation: The development and adoption of industrial methods of production which led to mass production, mechanisation, and factory systems. This was a significant shift from traditional handcrafts and agrarian ways of life.
  • Urbanisation: The movement of populations from rural areas to urban centres in search of employment, leading to the growth of cities and changes in living conditions and lifestyles.
  • Secularisation: A move away from religious and superstitious explanations of the world, with an increased focus on rationality, science, and secular ideologies.
  • Bureaucratisation: The creation and expansion of organised administrative systems and institutions to manage the complexities of modern states, economies, and societies.

These features collectively describe the transformation inherent in the concept of modernity, as opposed to the characteristics of earlier social orders such as feudalism, kingship, or medievalism. Feudalism typically relates to the social structures and hierarchies of the Middle Ages, Kingship refers to the rule by a king or monarch, and Medievalism pertains to the customs, culture, and thought of the medieval period.

question:
Consider the following events :

1. Launch of Non-Cooperation Movement

2. All-India Khilafat Conference in Delhi

3. Passing of the Rowlatt Act

4. Jallianwala Bagh incident

Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of the given events (earliest to latest)?

en.content:
Consider the following events :

1. Launch of Non-Cooperation Movement

2. All-India Khilafat Conference in Delhi

3. Passing of the Rowlatt Act

4. Jallianwala Bagh incident

Which one of the following is the correct chronological order of the given events (earliest to latest)?
en.explanation:
To determine the correct chronological order of the given events, let's review each event and its historical timeline:

1. Launch of Non-Cooperation Movement

The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in September 1920 as a response to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre and the Rowlatt Act. The movement sought to resist British rule through nonviolent means.

2. All-India Khilafat Conference in Delhi

The All-India Khilafat Conference was held in Delhi in November 1919. The Khilafat Movement sought to protect the Ottoman Caliphate and rally Muslim support against British rule.

3. Passing of the Rowlatt Act

The Rowlatt Act was passed by the British government in March 1919. This act allowed the government to imprison any person suspected of terrorism without trial, causing widespread unrest in India.

4. Jallianwala Bagh incident

The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred on April 13, 1919, in Amritsar. British troops, under the command of General Dyer, fired upon a peaceful gathering of Indians, resulting in the death of hundreds of unarmed civilians.

Now, arranging these events in chronological order:

  1. Passing of the Rowlatt Act (March 1919)
  2. Jallianwala Bagh incident (April 1919)
  3. All-India Khilafat Conference in Delhi (November 1919)
  4. Launch of Non-Cooperation Movement (September 1920)

Therefore, the correct chronological order of the given events is:

Option B:

  • 3, 4, 2, 1

D. Inam lands 4. Co-parcenary title holder and designated revenue payer in southern India
en.explanation:
To solve this matching problem, let's first break down the terms in List-I and match them with their appropriate descriptions in List-II:

List-I (System/Category)

  • Upari: This refers to a category of tenancy tenure held under the Marathas.
  • Pattadar: This represents peasants who are directly responsible for the payment of revenue.
  • Mirasidar: This is a co-parcenary title holder and designated revenue payer in southern India.
  • Inam lands: These are gifts of land or land revenue.

List-II (Description)

  • 1: Peasants directly responsible for the payment of revenue.
  • 2: Category of tenancy tenure held under the Marathas.
  • 3: Gifts of land or land revenue.
  • 4: Co-parcenary title holder and designated revenue payer in southern India.

Now, we can correctly match them:

Matching:

  • A. Upari → 2. Category of tenancy tenure held under the Marathas.
  • B. Pattadar → 1. Peasants directly responsible for the payment of revenue.
  • C. Mirasidar → 4. Co-parcenary title holder and designated revenue payer in southern India.
  • D. Inam lands → 3. Gifts of land or land revenue.

The correct choice would be:

A B C D
2 1 4 3

Therefore, the correct option is Option A.

question:
Which of the following statements about Vijayanagara king Krishnadevaraya's expeditions are correct?

1. In the early years of his reign he marched against the ruler of Orissa.

2. In 1520 CE, he captured the fort of Raichur from Ismail Adil Khan.

3. Krishnadevaraya restored Sultan Mahmud Shah to power and assumed the title of Yavana-rajya-sthapan-acharya.

Select the answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Which of the following statements about Vijayanagara king Krishnadevaraya's expeditions are correct?

1. In the early years of his reign he marched against the ruler of Orissa.

2. In 1520 CE, he captured the fort of Raichur from Ismail Adil Khan.

3. Krishnadevaraya restored Sultan Mahmud Shah to power and assumed the title of Yavana-rajya-sthapan-acharya.

Select the answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
To evaluate the correctness of the statements regarding the expeditions of the Vijayanagara king Krishnadevaraya, let’s analyze each statement individually.

Statement 1: In the early years of his reign he marched against the ruler of Orissa.

This statement is correct. Krishnadevaraya did launch campaigns against the Gajapati rulers of Orissa. This was part of his effort to consolidate and expand his kingdom.

Statement 2: In 1520 CE, he captured the fort of Raichur from Ismail Adil Khan.

This statement is also correct. The Battle of Raichur in 1520 CE was a significant military engagement where Krishnadevaraya defeated Ismail Adil Shah of the Bijapur Sultanate and captured the fort of Raichur.

Statement 3: Krishnadevaraya restored Sultan Mahmud Shah to power and assumed the title of Yavana-rajya-sthapan-acharya.

This statement is correct as well. Krishnadevaraya did restore Sultan Mahmud Shah, the Bahmani Sultan, to power and took the title of Yavana-rajya-sthapan-acharya (which roughly translates to "restorer of the Yavana kingdom").

Since all three statements are correct, the correct answer is:

Option C

1, 2 and 3

question:
Which of the following were features of the postal system of India as described by Ibn Battuta?

1. The foot post carries a ringing bell in one hand.

2. The horse post is called uluq, stationed at a distance of every four miles.

3. The foot post or dawa has one station at every four kos of distance.

4. The foot post is quicker than horse post.

Select the correct answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Which of the following were features of the postal system of India as described by Ibn Battuta?

1. The foot post carries a ringing bell in one hand.

2. The horse post is called uluq, stationed at a distance of every four miles.

3. The foot post or dawa has one station at every four kos of distance.

4. The foot post is quicker than horse post.

Select the correct answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
Ibn Battuta, a famous Moroccan traveler, documented several aspects of the Indian postal system during his travels in the 14th century. Based on his descriptions, let's analyze each statement:

Statement 1: The foot post carries a ringing bell in one hand.

Ibn Battuta noted that the foot post messengers, known locally, carried a bell in one hand which they rang to alert the recipient of an incoming message. This statement is correct.

Statement 2: The horse post is called uluq, stationed at a distance of every four miles.

This is accurate as per Ibn Battuta's records. The horse post, effective for quicker long-distance communication, was indeed termed uluq and stationed approximately every four miles.

Statement 3: The foot post or dawa has one station at every four kos of distance.

The unit kos refers to a traditional measure of distance in India, approximately 2 miles. According to Ibn Battuta, the dawa or foot post system did have stations placed at an interval of 4 kos. This statement is also accurate.

Statement 4: The foot post is quicker than horse post.

In practical terms, a horse post would generally be faster than a foot post. Therefore, this statement is incorrect.

So, the correct statements from the given options are 1, 2, and 3.

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option C

In practical terms, a horse post would generally be faster than a foot post. Therefore, this statement is incorrect.

So, the correct statements from the given options are 1, 2, and 3.

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option C

question:
Consider the following statements about the arrival of tobacco in India :

1. Tobacco plant arrived first in the Deccan.

2. Tobacco spread to northern India in the early years of the seventeenth century.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about the arrival of tobacco in India :

1. Tobacco plant arrived first in the Deccan.

2. Tobacco spread to northern India in the early years of the seventeenth century.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
To accurately determine which statements are correct about the arrival of tobacco in India, let’s examine the historical context of tobacco introduction and spread in India:

Statement 1: Tobacco plant arrived first in the Deccan.

Historically, tobacco was introduced to India by the Portuguese traders in the 16th century. The earliest evidence suggests that tobacco was first cultivated in the region of Deccan, which is located in the southern part of India. Hence, this statement is correct.

Statement 2: Tobacco spread to northern India in the early years of the seventeenth century.

Following its introduction in the Deccan, tobacco spread to various parts of India, including the northern regions, during the early 17th century. The spread of tobacco to northern India was facilitated by trade and colonial interactions. Thus, this statement is also correct.

Given the above analysis, both statements 1 and 2 are correct.

The correct answer is:

Option C

Both 1 and 2

question:
Consider the following Women's Associations and their important associates in early twentieth century India :

1. Women’s Indian Association of 1917 : Margaret Cousins

2. National Council of Women in India of 1925 : Sarala Devi Chaudhurani

3. Bharat Stree Mahamandal of 1910 : Lady Meherbai Tata

Which of the above pairs is/are correctly matched?

en.content:
Consider the following Women's Associations and their important associates in early twentieth century India :

1. Women’s Indian Association of 1917 : Margaret Cousins

2. National Council of Women in India of 1925 : Sarala Devi Chaudhurani

3. Bharat Stree Mahamandal of 1910 : Lady Meherbai Tata

Which of the above pairs is/are correctly matched?
en.explanation:
Let's examine the associations and their important associates mentioned in the question to determine which of the pairs are correctly matched :

1. Women's Indian Association of 1917 : Margaret Cousins

2. National Council of Women in India of 1925 : Sarala Devi Chaudhurani

3. Bharat Stree Mahamandal of 1910: Lady Meherbai Tata

Now, we'll verify each pair :

1. Women's Indian Association (WIA) was founded in 1917 by Annie Besant, Margaret Cousins, and others. Therefore, the given pair is correct as Margaret Cousins was one of the key figures.

2. The National Council of Women in India (NCWI) was formed in 1925. Sarala Devi Chaudhurani was indeed a prominent figure in the women's movement, but the NCWI was not specifically associated with her. She was more closely associated with the Bharat Stree Mahamandal. Therefore, this pair is incorrect.

3. The Bharat Stree Mahamandal was actually founded in 1910 by Sarala Devi Chaudhurani to promote women's education and rights. Lady Meherbai Tata was a notable Indian social worker and activist, but she was not associated with the founding of the Bharat Stree Mahamandal. Therefore, this pair is incorrect.

Based on this verification, the correctly matched pair is :

Option D : 1 only

question:
Consider the following statements about the issue of separate electorate during the early decades of the twentieth century in India as a means to secure adequate representation for the deprived social categories in the absence of universal adult franchise:

1. The All India Depressed Classes Association of M.C. Rajah was staunchly in favour of joint electorate.

2. The All India Depressed Classes Leaders’ Conference demanded separate electorate.

3. The Communal Award in September 1932 recognised the right to separate electorate for the 'untouchables'.

Which of the statements given above are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about the issue of separate electorate during the early decades of the twentieth century in India as a means to secure adequate representation for the deprived social categories in the absence of universal adult franchise:

1. The All India Depressed Classes Association of M.C. Rajah was staunchly in favour of joint electorate.

2. The All India Depressed Classes Leaders’ Conference demanded separate electorate.

3. The Communal Award in September 1932 recognised the right to separate electorate for the 'untouchables'.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option B: 2 and 3 only.

Let's break down the statements and understand why :

Statement 1 : The All India Depressed Classes Association of M.C. Rajah was staunchly in favour of joint electorate.

This statement is incorrect. M.C. Rajah's All India Depressed Classes Association was a prominent advocate for separate electorate for the "untouchables." They believed that separate electorates would guarantee their representation in the legislative bodies, as they felt that under a joint electorate system, they would be dominated by other communities.

Statement 2 : The All India Depressed Classes Leaders' Conference demanded separate electorate.

This statement is correct. The All India Depressed Classes Leaders' Conference, led by figures like Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, was a strong proponent of separate electorates for the "untouchables." They believed it was the only way to ensure their political and social advancement.

Statement 3 : The Communal Award in September 1932 recognised the right to separate electorate for the 'untouchables'.

This statement is also correct. The Communal Award, announced by the British government in 1932, granted separate electorates for the "untouchables" in the wake of the ongoing debate and pressure from organizations like the All India Depressed Classes Leaders' Conference. This award was a significant development in the fight for the political rights of the "untouchables" in India.

In conclusion, statements 2 and 3 are correct, while statement 1 is incorrect.

question:
Which of the following statements with regard to the famous 'silk routes' are correct?

1. Silk routes refer to East-bound European silk cargoes along these routes.

2. There were several silk routes, over land and by sea, joining together vast regions of Asia with Europe and northern Africa.

3. They are known to have existed since before the Christian Era and thrived almost till the fifteenth century.

4. Besides silk, Chinese pottery, textiles and spices from India and South-east Asia travelled the same route.

Select the answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Which of the following statements with regard to the famous 'silk routes' are correct?

1. Silk routes refer to East-bound European silk cargoes along these routes.

2. There were several silk routes, over land and by sea, joining together vast regions of Asia with Europe and northern Africa.

3. They are known to have existed since before the Christian Era and thrived almost till the fifteenth century.

4. Besides silk, Chinese pottery, textiles and spices from India and South-east Asia travelled the same route.

Select the answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
To determine which statements are correct about the 'silk routes,' let's examine each one individually :

1. Silk routes refer to East-bound European silk cargoes along these routes.
This statement is somewhat misleading because historically, the Silk Routes were primarily known for the trade from the East (Asia) to the West (Europe), and not specifically for the East-bound movement of European silk.

2. There were several silk routes, over land and by sea, joining together vast regions of Asia with Europe and northern Africa.
This statement is correct. The Silk Routes consisted of various interconnected trade routes, both overland and maritime, that connected different parts of Asia to Europe and northern Africa.

3. They are known to have existed since before the Christian Era and thrived almost till the fifteenth century.
This statement is also correct. The Silk Routes were in use for many centuries, beginning before the Common Era and continuing to be vital trade routes until around the fifteenth century.

4. Besides silk, Chinese pottery, textiles and spices from India and South-east Asia travelled the same route.
This statement is correct. The Silk Routes were not limited to the trade of silk. Various other goods, including Chinese pottery, textiles, and spices from India and South-east Asia, were traded along these routes.

Based on this analysis, the statements 2, 3, and 4 are correct. Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option C

2, 3 and 4

question:
Consider the following statements about Ajanta paintings :

1. The surface of the rock was coated with clay mixed with rice husk and gum and a coat of lime was applied over this.

2. The outline drawing was done first on the lime coating and colours were added subsequently.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about Ajanta paintings :

1. The surface of the rock was coated with clay mixed with rice husk and gum and a coat of lime was applied over this.

2. The outline drawing was done first on the lime coating and colours were added subsequently.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
The Ajanta paintings, which are one of the finest examples of ancient Indian art, particularly involve a sophisticated method of preparation and painting. Let's analyze each statement in detail :

Statement 1 : The surface of the rock was coated with clay mixed with rice husk and gum and a coat of lime was applied over this.

This statement is correct. The process of preparing the rock surface for painting at Ajanta involved first applying a mixture of clay, rice husk, and some binding material like gum. Over this base, a coat of lime plaster was applied to create a smooth and workable surface for the artists to paint on.

Statement 2 : The outline drawing was done first on the lime coating and colours were added subsequently.

This statement is also correct. After preparing the surface with a lime coating, the artists would draw the outlines of the images they intended to paint. Following this step, they filled in the outlines with various pigments, adding colors and details to complete the artwork.

Since both statements 1 and 2 accurately describe the process involved in creating the Ajanta paintings, the correct answer is :

C

Both 1 and 2

1. चट्टान की सतह को चावल की भूसी और गोंद मिश्रित मिट्टी से लेपित किया गया था और इस पर चूने का लेप लगाया गया था ।
2. पहले चूने के लेप पर रूपरेखा खींची गई और उसके बाद उसमें रंगों का...

solution:
The Ajanta paintings, which are one of the finest examples of ancient Indian art, particularly involve a sophisticated method of preparation and painting. Let's analyze each statement in detail :

Statement 1 : The surface of the rock was coated with clay mixed with rice husk and gum and a coat of lime was applied over this.

This statement is correct. The process of preparing the rock surface for painting at Ajanta involved first applying a mixture of clay, rice husk, and some binding material like gum. Over this base, a coat of lime plaster was applied to create a smooth and workable surface for the artists to paint on.

Statement 2 : The outline drawing was done first on the lime coating and colours were added subsequently.

This statement is also correct. After preparing the surface with a lime coating, the artists would draw the outlines of the images they intended to paint. Following this step, they filled in the outlines with various pigments, adding colors and details to complete the artwork.

Since both statements 1 and 2 accurately describe the process involved in creating the Ajanta paintings, the correct answer is :

C

Both 1 and 2

options: Identifying with a variety of deities
question: Which one of the following was NOT a way in which kings in ancient India attempted to claim a higher status?

en.content: Which one of the following was NOT a way in which kings in ancient India attempted to claim a higher status?

options.content: Identifying with a variety of deities

en.explanation:
The answer is Option C: Imposing high taxes. Here's why:

Ancient Indian kings used various strategies to establish and maintain their authority and elevate their status. Let's examine each option:

Option A: Identifying with a variety of deities

This was a common practice. Kings often claimed divine lineage or a special relationship with particular deities. This served to legitimize their rule and connect them with powerful cosmic forces. For example, the Mauryan emperor Ashoka claimed to be a follower of Buddhism and promoted the religion.

Option B: Assuming pompous titles

Titles like "Maharaja" (Great King), "Chakravarti" (Universal Ruler), or "Samrat" (Emperor) were used to emphasize the king's power and prestige. These titles helped distinguish them from ordinary rulers and projected an image of grandeur and authority.

Option C: Imposing high taxes

While kings did levy taxes, this was primarily for funding the state, maintaining armies, and supporting infrastructure, not necessarily for claiming higher status. Taxes were a necessary part of governance, not a tool for elevating the king's personal position above the people.

Option D: Conquests and annexation of territories

Expansion through military conquest was a powerful way for kings to increase their power, wealth, and territory. This demonstrated their military prowess and strength, enhancing their prestige and influence both within their realm and among neighboring kingdoms.

In summary, while kings used taxes to manage their kingdoms, the primary ways they sought to claim a higher status were through religious associations, grand titles, and military victories.

options:
The Bhakti movement avoided traditions that promoted diversity
In northern India, the Bhakti movement revolved around the worship of Rama and Krishna
The Bhakti movement relied on the devotion of God, both with and without attributes
While Tulsidas represented the tradition of devotion with attributes, Sant Kabir followed the path of devotion without attributes
question: Which one of the following statements about the Bhakti movement is NOT correct?

en.content: Which one of the following statements about the Bhakti movement is NOT correct?

options.content: The Bhakti movement avoided traditions that promoted diversity
In northern India, the Bhakti movement revolved around the worship of Rama and Krishna
The Bhakti movement relied on the devotion of God, both with and without attributes
While Tulsidas represented the tradition of devotion with attributes, Sant Kabir followed the path of devotion without attributes

en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option A. Here's why:

The Bhakti movement was known for its embrace of diversity, not its avoidance. Here's a breakdown of the key aspects of the Bhakti movement and why the other options are correct:

Option B: This statement is correct. The Bhakti movement in northern India, particularly in the Hindi belt, was deeply influenced by the worship of Rama and Krishna. These deities were central to the devotional practices and the literature that emerged from this period.

Option C: This statement is also correct. The Bhakti movement encompassed both devotional practices focused on God with attributes (Saguna Bhakti) and devotion to God without attributes (Nirguna Bhakti).

  • Saguna Bhakti: This involved worshipping God in a form, with specific qualities and attributes. Think of Rama, Krishna, Vishnu, etc.
  • Nirguna Bhakti: This focused on the formless, attributeless aspect of the divine. It emphasized the unity of all beings and the realization of the divine within oneself.

Option D: This statement is correct. Tulsidas, known for his epic "Ramcharitmanas," is a prime example of Saguna Bhakti, focusing on the worship of Rama with specific attributes. Sant Kabir, on the other hand, emphasized Nirguna Bhakti, advocating for the unity of all beings and the search for the formless divine.

Option A: This statement is incorrect. The Bhakti movement was incredibly diverse, welcoming people from all social backgrounds, castes, and regions. It celebrated different forms of worship and spiritual paths, promoting inclusivity and challenging rigid social hierarchies.

hi.content: भक्ति आंदोलन के बारे में निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है?

options.content: भक्ति आंदोलन ने विविधता को बढ़ावा देने वाली परंपराओं से परहेज किया
उत्तर भारत में भक्ति आंदोलन राम और कृष्ण की पूजा के इर्द-गिर्द घूमता था
भक्ति आंदोलन ईश्वर की भक्ति पर निर्भर था, चाहे वह गुण सहित हो या गुण रहित।
तुलसीदास ने गुण-सहित भक्ति की परंपरा का प्रतिनिधित्व किया, जबकि संत कबीर ने निर्गुण भक्ति का मार्ग अपनाया

options: Bands of revolutionaries moving around to terrorise people who did not participate in the revolution
The royal forces punishing people who participated in the revolution
Trial and beheading persons considered enemies of the republic by the revolutionary tribunal
The general atmosphere of insecurity perceived by people after the beheading of the king
question: The 'Reign of Terror' in revolutionary France referred to

en.content: The 'Reign of Terror' in revolutionary France referred to

options.content: Bands of revolutionaries moving around to terrorise people who did not participate in the revolution
The royal forces punishing people who participated in the revolution
Trial and beheading persons considered enemies of the republic by the revolutionary tribunal
The general atmosphere of insecurity perceived by people after the beheading of the king

en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option C: Trial and beheading persons considered enemies of the republic by the revolutionary tribunal.

Here's why:

The Reign of Terror, a period in the French Revolution from 1793 to 1794, was marked by extreme violence and political repression. It was characterized by the following:

  • The Revolutionary Tribunal: This court was established to try individuals accused of treason against the French Republic. The accusations were often based on flimsy evidence or political motives.
  • Mass Executions: The Tribunal often handed down death sentences, and the guillotine became the primary method of execution. Thousands of people were executed during this period.
  • Fear and Suspicion: The Reign of Terror created a climate of fear and suspicion. People were afraid to express their opinions or associate with those who might be seen as enemies of the republic.

Let's examine why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: While there were instances of violence and unrest during the French Revolution, the Reign of Terror was more organized and systematic, with the Revolutionary Tribunal acting as the legal body for executions.
  • Option B: The royal forces had been largely dismantled by the time the Reign of Terror began. The executions were carried out by the revolutionaries themselves.
  • Option D: While the beheading of King Louis XVI did create a sense of uncertainty, the Reign of Terror was a specific period of organized violence orchestrated by the revolutionary government.

The Reign of Terror was a dark chapter in French history, marked by its systematic use of violence and repression to control dissent and consolidate the power of the revolutionary government. It's important to understand this period not just as a time of chaos but as a deliberate policy implemented by the Committee of Public Safety, a powerful body that emerged during the revolution.

options: From a continent of creditors it turned into a continent of debtors
Soldiers came to be placed higher in social status than civilians
Popular support decreased for conservative dictatorship which had come into being recently
National honour occupied centre stage in the public sphere
question: In which one of the following ways did the First World War Not impact Europe?

en.content: In which one of the following ways did the First World War Not impact Europe?

options.content: From a continent of creditors it turned into a continent of debtors
Soldiers came to be placed higher in social status than civilians
Popular support decreased for conservative dictatorship which had come into being recently
National honour occupied centre stage in the public sphere

en.explanation:
The First World War took place from 28th July 1914 to 11th November 1918 in which Army, Navy and Air forces of about 30 countries participated. The international conflict principally between Central Powers (mainly Germany, Austria-Hungary, and Turkey) against the Allies (mainly France, Great Britain, Russia, Italy, Japan resulted in the deaths of millions of soldiers and massacres around the world. Europe was badly affected by this war.

  • The European economy had collapsed and it transformed from world's largest creditors to the world's debtors.

  • National honor occupied center stage in the public sphere in Europe after First World War.

  • Popular support for conservative dictatorships in Europe increased after the First World War because people demanded self-protection and security.

  • Soldiers come to be placed higher in social status than civilians. They are regarded as "Heroes of the War".

question: "...the immediate ending of British rule in India is an urgent necessity..." was a resolution of

en.content: "...the immediate ending of British rule in India is an urgent necessity..." was a resolution of
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option B: Quit India Movement.

The "Quit India Movement", launched in 1942, was a mass movement demanding immediate independence from British rule. The resolution calling for the immediate ending of British rule was passed at the All India Congress Committee meeting in Bombay on August 8, 1942. Mahatma Gandhi, the leader of the Indian National Congress, gave the call for "Do or Die" which resonated with the Indian people. This movement was marked by widespread civil disobedience, strikes, and arrests, highlighting the urgency and intensity of the demand for independence.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

Option A: Non-Cooperation Movement: This movement, launched in 1920, aimed to achieve Swaraj (self-rule) through non-violent means but did not specifically demand an immediate end to British rule. It focused on boycotting British goods and institutions.

Option C: Individual Satyagraha Movement: This movement, launched in 1940, involved individuals undertaking acts of civil disobedience against specific British policies. It was not a mass movement aiming for an immediate end to British rule.

Option D: Civil Disobedience Movement: This movement, launched in 1930, was a significant step towards independence, but it focused on resisting specific laws like the Salt Tax. While it aimed to pressure the British government, it did not explicitly demand an immediate end to British rule.

Therefore, only the Quit India Movement explicitly demanded the immediate ending of British rule in India, making it the correct answer.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding Sanchi and its archaeological findings:

1. When Sanchi was discovered in 1818, three of its four gateways were still standing, the fourth was lying on the spot where it had fallen

2. Animals depicted at Sanchi include elephants, horses, mon- keys and cattle

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements regarding Sanchi and its archaeological findings:

1. When Sanchi was discovered in 1818, three of its four gateways were still standing, the fourth was lying on the spot where it had fallen

2. Animals depicted at Sanchi include elephants, horses, mon- keys and cattle

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option C: Both 1 and 2.

Here's a breakdown of why both statements are correct:

Statement 1:

This statement is accurate. When the British officer, Taylor, discovered Sanchi in 1818, three of the four ornate gateways were still standing, while the fourth had collapsed and lay on the ground. The discovery of Sanchi was a significant event, bringing to light the rich architectural and artistic heritage of ancient India.

Statement 2:

This statement is also correct. The sculptures at Sanchi are known for their detailed depictions of various animals, including elephants, horses, monkeys, and cattle. These animals often appear in scenes of daily life, religious narratives, and symbolic representations. The presence of these animals in the art of Sanchi provides insights into the cultural beliefs and practices of the time.

Therefore, both statements are correct, making Option C the correct answer.

Therefore, both statements are correct, making Option C the correct answer.

question: Chandimangala, a sixteenth century text, was composed by

en.content: Chandimangala, a sixteenth century text, was composed by
en.explanation:
Mukundarama Chakravarti composed the Chandimangala epic in the late 16th century. He was originally a Bengali poet who became very popular after writing the epic poem Chandimangal. The Chandimangala/Mangalkavya, considered one of the most prominent works of the 16th century which is a sub-genre of medieval Bengali literature.

So, the correct answer is:

Option D : Mukundaram Chakrabarti

  • Hema Saraswati was one of the earliest known Assamese writers, best known for his poem Prahlad Charitra, the earliest known poetic work in the Assamese language.

  • He was a court poet under the patronage of King Durlabh Narayan of the Kamata Kingdom of Kamtapur, who also patronized his contemporary Harivar Vipra.

  • Kaviraj Madhav Kandali was an Indian poet from Assam.

  • He is renowned as one of the famous poets of the pre-Shankar era.

  • His Saptakanda Ramayana is considered the earliest translation of the Ramayana into the Indo-Aryan language, Assamese.

  • Sant Tukaram Maharaj, also known as Tuka, Tukobaraya, Tukoba, was a Hindu Marathi saint of the "Varkari sect" in Dehu village, Maharashtra in the 17th century.

  • He was a devoted follower of Lord Pandurang of Pandharpur.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding the royal center in Vijayanagara Empire:

1. The royal center was located in the south-western part of the settlement

2. It included over 60 temples

3. About 30 building complexes at the site have been identified as palaces

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements regarding the royal center in Vijayanagara Empire:

1. The royal center was located in the south-western part of the settlement

2. It included over 60 temples

3. About 30 building complexes at the site have been identified as palaces

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option C: 1, 2 and 3.

Here's a breakdown of why each statement is correct:

Statement 1: The royal center was located in the south-western part of the settlement

This statement is true. The royal center of the Vijayanagara Empire, known as the "Royal Enclosure", was indeed located in the southwestern part of the city of Hampi. This area was meticulously planned and included palaces, temples, and administrative buildings.

Statement 2: It included over 60 temples

This statement is also true. The royal center was a hub of religious activity, and it housed a significant number of temples. Estimates suggest there were over 60 temples within the Royal Enclosure, showcasing the importance of religion in the Vijayanagara administration and society.

Statement 3: About 30 building complexes at the site have been identified as palaces

This statement is correct as well. Extensive archaeological investigations have revealed numerous building complexes within the royal center that are believed to have served as palaces for the Vijayanagara rulers and their families. The architectural grandeur and the sheer number of these palaces reflect the wealth and power of the empire.

Therefore, all three statements are accurate regarding the royal center of the Vijayanagara Empire.

question:
Consider the following statements:

1. Terracotta models of the plough have been found at sites in Cholistan and at Banawali (Haryana).

2. There is evidence of a ploughed field at Kalibangan associated with the mature phase of the Harappan civilization.

3. The ploughed field at Kalibangan had two sets of furrows crossing at right angles to each other suggesting two different crops were grown together in it.

Which of the statements given above are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements:

1. Terracotta models of the plough have been found at sites in Cholistan and at Banawali (Haryana).

2. There is evidence of a ploughed field at Kalibangan associated with the mature phase of the Harappan civilization.

3. The ploughed field at Kalibangan had two sets of furrows crossing at right angles to each other suggesting two different crops were grown together in it.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
en.explanation:
To determine which statements are correct, let's analyze them one by one concerning the archaeological findings related to the Harappan civilization:

1. Terracotta models of the plough have been found at sites in Cholistan and at Banawali (Haryana).

This statement is correct. Archaeological excavations have discovered terracotta models of ploughs at both these sites, indicating the use of ploughing tools in agriculture during the Harappan civilization.

2. There is evidence of a ploughed field at Kalibangan associated with the mature phase of the Harappan civilization.

This statement is also correct. Kalibangan, located in present-day Rajasthan, is one of the significant Harappan sites where evidence of ploughed fields has been discovered, which dates back to the mature phase of the Harappan civilization.

3. The ploughed field at Kalibangan had two sets of furrows crossing at right angles to each other suggesting two different crops were grown together in it.

This statement is true as well. The finding of furrows intersecting at right angles in Kalibangan suggests the practice of mixed cropping or sequential cropping, where two different types of crops were possibly cultivated together.

Given this analysis, the correct answer is:

Option A

1, 2 and 3

question:
Which of the following statements about the technological innovations during the Industrial Revolutions are correct?

1. Mule was the nickname for a machine invented in 1779, that allowed the spinning of strong and fine yarn.

2. Water Frame, invented in 1769 by Richard Arkwright, made it possible to weave pure cotton clothes.

3. Power loom, invented by Edmund Cartwright in 1787, could be used to weave any kind of materials.

Select the correct answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Which of the following statements about the technological innovations during the Industrial Revolutions are correct?

1. Mule was the nickname for a machine invented in 1779, that allowed the spinning of strong and fine yarn.

2. Water Frame, invented in 1769 by Richard Arkwright, made it possible to weave pure cotton clothes.

3. Power loom, invented by Edmund Cartwright in 1787, could be used to weave any kind of materials.

Select the correct answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
1. Mule was the nickname for a machine invented in 1779, that allowed the spinning of strong and fine yarn.

  • Correct. The spinning mule, invented by Samuel Crompton in 1779, combined features of the spinning jenny and the water frame, allowing the production of strong and fine yarn.

  1. Water Frame, invented in 1769 by Richard Arkwright, made it possible to weave pure cotton clothes.

  • Correct. While the Water Frame, invented by Richard Arkwright in 1769, was primarily used for spinning cotton into thread or yarn, it was a significant invention that contributed to the ability to produce pure cotton clothes by providing a reliable source of cotton thread for weaving.

  1. Power loom, invented by Edmund Cartwright in 1787, could be used to weave any kind of materials.

  • Correct. The power loom, invented by Edmund Cartwright in 1787, mechanized the process of weaving and could be used to weave various materials, revolutionizing the textile industry.

Therefore, all three statements are indeed correct. Thus, the correct answer is:

Option B: 1, 2 and 3

question: Which one of the following Ashokan Minor Rock Edicts, contains the personal name of the king - Ashoka?

en.content: Which one of the following Ashokan Minor Rock Edicts, contains the personal name of the king - Ashoka?
en.explanation:
The Ashokan Minor Rock Edict that contains the personal name of the king, Ashoka, is found at Maski. This is an important historical discovery as it provided clear evidence linking the title Devanampriya (which means "Beloved of the Gods") to King Ashoka himself. Most inscriptions refer to him by his titles rather than his personal name, making the Maski edict a significant artifact in confirming the identity of the emperor.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option A: Maski

question:
Arrange the following in correct chronological order

1. Charak Samhita

2. Bharat's Natyashastra

3. Sangam Literature

4. Ashtadhyayi

Select the correct answer using the code given below:

en.content:
Arrange the following in correct chronological order

1. Charak Samhita

2. Bharat's Natyashastra

3. Sangam Literature

4. Ashtadhyayi

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
en.explanation:
To arrange these ancient Indian texts in their correct chronological order, we need to first establish the approximate dates or periods of their composition:

1. Charak Samhita - This is an ancient Indian Ayurvedic text, traditionally attributed to Charaka. It is believed to have been composed around the 1st century CE.

2. Bharat's Natyashastra - This is an ancient Indian treatise on the performing arts, particularly theatre, dance, and music. It is believed to date back to between 200 BCE and 200 CE, with the most accepted scholars' estimation being around the 2nd century CE.

3. Sangam Literature - This body of classical Tamil literature was created over a period stretching from roughly 300 BCE to 300 CE. The exact dates for individual works in the Sangam corpus can vary, but many are placed around the 1st to 3rd centuries CE.

4. Ashtadhyayi - This is a Sanskrit grammar text written by the ancient Indian scholar Panini. It is generally dated to approximately the 4th century BCE.

From the above information, we can establish the following chronological order:

  • Ashtadhyayi (4th century BCE)
  • Sangam Literature (300 BCE to 300 CE)
  • Charak Samhita (1st century CE)
  • Bharat's Natyashastra (2nd century CE)

Thus, the correct chronological order is:

4 - 3 - 1 - 2

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: 4 - 3 - 1 - 2

question:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Bhoodan Movement:

1. Vinoba Bhave organized "The Sarvodaya Samaj' to take up the work of non-violent transformation in India

2. Jayaprakash Narayan withdrew from active politics to join the Bhoodan Movement in 1953

3. In the wake of the Bhoodan Movement the first donation of land was made in the village of Pochampalli in Telangana

How many of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Bhoodan Movement:

1. Vinoba Bhave organized "The Sarvodaya Samaj' to take up the work of non-violent transformation in India

2. Jayaprakash Narayan withdrew from active politics to join the Bhoodan Movement in 1953

3. In the wake of the Bhoodan Movement the first donation of land was made in the village of Pochampalli in Telangana

How many of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
To answer the question, let us analyze each of the statements given with reference to the Bhoodan Movement:

1. Vinoba Bhave organized "The Sarvodaya Samaj" to take up the work of non-violent transformation in India:

This statement is correct. Vinoba Bhave was greatly influenced by Mahatma Gandhi's principles and ideals. The Sarvodaya Samaj was indeed organized by Vinoba Bhave, and it aimed at the non-violent transformation of society by promoting wealth distribution and social equity.

2. Jayaprakash Narayan withdrew from active politics to join the Bhoodan Movement in 1953:

This statement is also correct. Jayaprakash Narayan, a prominent Indian independence activist and political leader, left active politics in the early 1950s. He joined the Bhoodan Movement in 1953 and supported Vinoba Bhave's mission of land donation and redistribution for the welfare of the rural poor.

3. In the wake of the Bhoodan Movement, the first donation of land was made in the village of Pochampalli in Telangana:

This statement is correct as well. The Bhoodan Movement was initiated by Vinoba Bhave in 1951, and the first donation of land indeed took place in the village of Pochampalli in the state of Telangana. The correct answer is:

Option C: 3

question:
Consider the following statements about gallantry awards in India:

1. Post-independence, first three gallantry awards were instituted on 26th January, 1950

2. The first three gallantry awards were Ashoka Chakra Class-I, Ashoka Chakra Class-II and Ashoka Chakra Class-III

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about gallantry awards in India:

1. Post-independence, first three gallantry awards were instituted on 26th January, 1950

2. The first three gallantry awards were Ashoka Chakra Class-I, Ashoka Chakra Class-II and Ashoka Chakra Class-III

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
Let's analyze the given statements about gallantry awards in India:

1. Post-independence, first three gallantry awards were instituted on 26th January, 1950

2. The first three gallantry awards were Ashoka Chakra Class-I, Ashoka Chakra Class-II and Ashoka Chakra Class-III

Statement 1: This statement is correct. The first three gallantry awards instituted in post-independence India on 26th January, 1950 were the Param Vir Chakra, the Maha Vir Chakra, and the Vir Chakra.

Statement 2: This statement is incorrect. The first three gallantry awards were not Ashoka Chakra Class-I, Ashoka Chakra Class-II, and Ashoka Chakra Class-III. These are peacetime gallantry awards, which were instituted later. The Param Vir Chakra, the Maha Vir Chakra, and the Vir Chakra are the correct first three gallantry awards.

Hence, the correct option is:

Option A: 1 only

question: 'Sengol', installed in the new Parliament building of India, was seen as a symbol of the path of service, duty and nation in which Empire of the past?

en.content: 'Sengol', installed in the new Parliament building of India, was seen as a symbol of the path of service, duty and nation in which Empire of the past?
en.explanation:
The 'Sengol,' installed in the new Parliament building of India, was seen as a symbol of the path of service, duty, and nation in the Chola Empire. The Chola Empire, renowned for its governance, culture, and contributions to art and architecture, used such symbols to represent the transfer of power and the ethical duties of rulers. Hence, the correct answer is:

Option D: Chola Empire

question: Which one among the following statements about the Mansabdari system is correct?

en.content: Which one among the following statements about the Mansabdari system is correct?

options.content: All army troopers were allotted mansabs.
Mansabs were usually assigned on the basis of ancestry.
Position and salary of mansabdars were indicated by a numerical designation called zat.
Mansabdars were never paid in cash.

en.explanation:
The correct statement about the Mansabdari system is:

Option C: Position and salary of mansabdars were indicated by a numerical designation called zat.

The Mansabdari system was a bureaucratic administration system introduced by Mughal Emperor Akbar in India. The term zat denotes the rank and salary scale of the mansabdar, which was an important part of the system.

hi.content: मनसबदारी प्रथा (व्यवस्था) के बारे में निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही है?

मनसबदारों के पद और वेतन को ज़ात (zat) नामक संख्यात्मक पदनाम द्वारा सूचित किया जाता था।
